digest.component_or_area: op-cert trusted gate service deployment (AWS IaC + compose/nginx wiring)
propositions: Extend the existing Terraform to declare an API Gateway HTTP API v2 with AWS_IAM (SigV4) auth over TLS, integrating to the box's https nginx origin with a static X-Opcert-Guard header, adding zero new fixed-monthly-cost resources.
Restrict execute-api:Invoke to a single IaC-created rebar-opcert-admin role whose trust policy comes from opcert_admin_principal_arns, with no other principal granted Invoke.

Store the passphrase-free Ed25519 key and the origin-guard value as SSM SecureStrings under /rebar/prod/, the key guarded by lifecycle ignore_changes=[value] and the guard fully Terraform-managed via random_password.
Wire the opcert compose service and a fail-closed guarded /opcert/ nginx location, with materialize-opcert-guard.sh rendering the guard from SSM into the host nginx map before docker compose up.
Deploy via authenticated AWS CLI, seed the key, and pin the environment's public key and env_id into trusted_environments.yaml using the log-position era anchor.
Prove the deployed endpoint end-to-end: a SigV4-signed job round-trip succeeds while unsigned requests and guardless direct-to-origin requests are rejected with 403.
